name: National Means Cum Merit Scholarship (NMMS)

eligibility.qualification: Passed Class 8 from Government/Govt-aided school
eligibility.stateConditions: Must be studying in a Government/Govt-aided school

applicationProcess: Fill in personal, academic, and bank details
Submit and take printout of application
renewalConditions: Minimum 55% marks in annual exam; continue in Govt school

name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC Students

eligibility.qualification: Studying in Class 9 or 10
eligibility.stateConditions: Must be a domicile of the applying state

applicationProcess: Fill personal, academic and bank details
renewalConditions: Promotion to next class; maintain attendance

name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for ST Students

eligibility.qualification: Studying in Class 9 or 10
eligibility.stateConditions: Must be domicile of respective state; Tribal Development Dept.

amount: Day Scholar: ₹150/month; Hosteller: ₹350/month + books and ad-hoc allowances
applicationProcess: Visit NSP portal or state tribal dept website
Upload ST certificate and income proof
renewalConditions: Pass in annual exam; tribal status unchanged

name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for OBC Students

eligibility.qualification: Studying in Class 9 or 10 in a Govt/Govt-aided school
eligibility.stateConditions: OBC non-creamy layer; state domicile required

applicationProcess: Attach documents and submit before deadline
renewalConditions: Annual renewal on promotion; income limit maintained

name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for Minorities

eligibility.qualification: Studying in Class 9 or 10
eligibility.category: Muslim, Sikh, Christian, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi
eligibility.stateConditions: Must belong to notified minority community

amount: Day Scholar: ₹1,000/year maintenance + ₹500 ad-hoc; Hosteller: ₹3,000/year + ₹500 ad-hoc
renewalConditions: 50% marks in annual exam; fresh application each year

eligibility.qualification: Girls studying in Class 9 to 12
eligibility.category: Muslim, Sikh, Christian, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi (Minority)
eligibility.stateConditions: Recognized school in India

name: Rajarshi Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj Scholarship (Pre-Matric)

eligibility.qualification: Studying in Class 9 or 10 in Maharashtra
eligibility.stateConditions: Maharashtra domicile; must be from a recognized school in Maharashtra

renewalConditions: Annual renewal; promotion to next class

name: Dr. Punjabrao Deshmukh Vasatigruh Nirvah Bhatta Yojana

eligibility.qualification: Students residing in hostels away from home
eligibility.stateConditions: Must reside in approved hostel in Maharashtra; domicile required

amount: ₹3,000 – ₹24,000 per year depending on category and level
applicationProcess: Login and select 'Vasatigruh Nirvah Bhatta Yojana'
renewalConditions: Annual; staying in hostel and passing exams

eligibility.qualification: SC/OBC students studying in Class 9-10 in Maharashtra
eligibility.stateConditions: Maharashtra domicile; recognized school

name: Tribal Development Department Scholarships (Maharashtra)

eligibility.qualification: ST students at various education levels in Maharashtra
eligibility.stateConditions: Maharashtra tribal domicile; Tribal Research & Training Institute (TRTI)

amount: Pre-matric: ₹150-₹350/month; Post-matric: up to ₹1,200/month + tuition fee
renewalConditions: Annual renewal; must pass each year

eligibility.qualification: Minority students in Class 9-10 in Maharashtra
eligibility.category: Muslim, Sikh, Christian, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi
eligibility.stateConditions: Maharashtra domicile; recognized school

name: Vidyadhan Scholarship

eligibility.qualification: Passed Class 10 with distinction OR studying in 11th/12th
eligibility.stateConditions: Available in: Karnataka, Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Kerala, Odisha

renewalConditions: Annual renewal based on performance; maintain good academic record
stateApplicability: Karnataka, Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, AP, Telangana, Kerala, Odisha

name: Buddy4Study Pre-Matric Scholarships

eligibility.qualification: Varies by individual scholarship on the platform
eligibility.stateConditions: State-specific and all-India schemes available

applicationProcess: Upload documents as required per scholarship

name: SOF Olympiad Scholarships

eligibility.qualification: Students in Class 1-12 studying in CBSE/ICSE/State Board schools
eligibility.minPercentage: No minimum; based on Olympiad exam performance
eligibility.stateConditions: School must be registered with SOF

amount: Cash prizes: ₹50,000 – ₹1,00,000 for top ranks; gold medals and gifts
applicationProcess: School registers with SOF at sofworld.org
Appear for SOF Olympiad exam (NSO, IMO, NCO, IEO)
Results declared; winners receive awards directly

name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship (CSSS)

eligibility.qualification: Passed Class 12 from recognized board; enrolled in regular UG/PG course
eligibility.minPercentage: 80th percentile or above in Class 12 board exam
eligibility.stateConditions: Must be studying in regular (non-distance) college

renewalConditions: 60% marks in previous year exam; continued enrollment in regular course

name: INSPIRE Scholarship (DST)

eligibility.qualification: Top 1% in Class 12 board OR qualified JEE/NEET in top 10,000 rank; enrolled in B.Sc/BS/Int.MSc in Natural/Basic Sciences
eligibility.minPercentage: Top 1% in board exam OR JEE/NEET top rank
eligibility.stateConditions: Must pursue Natural/Basic sciences (Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Math) – NOT Engineering/Medical

applicationProcess: Upload marks proof and enrollment letter
renewalConditions: 55% marks in previous year; continue in science stream

name: Prime Minister's Scholarship Scheme (PMSS)
type: Central Government (Ministry of Home Affairs / Department of Ex-Servicemen)

eligibility.qualification: Wards/widows of Ex-Servicemen (Army/Navy/Air Force) or CAPF personnel; enrolled in 1st year of professional degree
eligibility.familyIncomeLimit: No income limit (priority to those with lower pay)
eligibility.stateConditions: No state restriction; national scheme

applicationProcess: Register and fill PMSS online application
Submit and track status on KSB portal

name: Post-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST/OBC/Minority (Central)

eligibility.qualification: Passed Class 10; studying in post-matric level (11th and above)
eligibility.category: SC, ST, OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), Minority communities
eligibility.stateConditions: State domicile required; apply through respective state government

amount: Varies by level: Day Scholar ₹230-₹550/month; Hosteller ₹380-₹1,200/month + tuition fee reimbursement
renewalConditions: Annual renewal; pass in exam; income within limit

name: Rajarshi Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj Scholarship – EBC (Maharashtra)

eligibility.qualification: Students studying in 11th class and above in Maharashtra (Open/General EBC category)
eligibility.stateConditions: Maharashtra domicile; must be in recognized Maharashtra institution

amount: Tuition fees + exam fees + maintenance allowance (varies by institution type)
renewalConditions: Annual renewal; pass in previous year exam

name: Dr. Punjabrao Deshmukh Scholarship (Post-Matric Maharashtra)

eligibility.qualification: Students from SC/ST/OBC/VJNT/SBC categories in Maharashtra staying in hostels
eligibility.stateConditions: Residing in approved government or recognized hostel in Maharashtra

amount: ₹3,000 – ₹24,000 per year maintenance allowance based on category and level

name: Tuition Fee & Exam Fee Scheme – TFWS (Maharashtra)

eligibility.qualification: Admitted to govt/govt-aided/unaided engineering, pharmacy or diploma college in Maharashtra through CAP round
eligibility.minPercentage: No percentage cut-off; depends on merit in CET
eligibility.category: Open/General category (not from reserved category – those have separate schemes)
eligibility.stateConditions: Must be admitted through Maharashtra DTE/MSBTE centralized admission process

amount: Full Tuition Fee + Exam Fee waiver (covers entire technical degree fee in govt/aided institutes)
applicationProcess: Automatically considered during DTE CAP round if income ≤ ₹8L
Apply separately on DTE/MahaDBT portal if not auto-covered
Institution forwards to DTE for processing
renewalConditions: Annual; clear exams with no backlog (some institutions allow 1 backlog)

name: AICTE Pragati Scholarship (Girls in Technical Education)

eligibility.qualification: Girl students admitted to 1st year of AICTE-approved Diploma or Degree program
eligibility.minPercentage: Based on merit in board/CET; typically top 1/3rd of class required for renewal
eligibility.stateConditions: Must be in an AICTE-approved institution; one family – one girl policy (max 2 girls per family)

amount: ₹50,000 per year (₹30,000 tuition + ₹20,000 incidental) – up to ₹50,000/yr
renewalConditions: Top 1/3rd of class; no year back

eligibility.qualification: Students with 40%+ disability admitted in AICTE-approved Diploma or Degree
eligibility.stateConditions: Must be in AICTE-approved institution

eligibility.qualification: Students who lost both parents OR whose parent died in COVID-19; admitted to AICTE-approved institution
eligibility.category: Orphans, COVID-19 affected, armed forces/CAPF martyrs' children
eligibility.stateConditions: AICTE-approved institution mandatory

name: Reliance Foundation Undergraduate Scholarship

eligibility.qualification: Passed Class 12 with 60%+; enrolled in 1st year UG (STEM or Humanities)
eligibility.stateConditions: Available all India; priority to underprivileged

amount: Up to ₹2,00,000 per year (tuition + living expenses) + mentorship
applicationProcess: Write SOP explaining goals and financial need
renewalConditions: 60% marks annually; active participation in mentoring

name: Tata Capital Pankh Scholarship

eligibility.qualification: Students from Classes 11-12 or pursuing higher education (ITI/Diploma/UG)
eligibility.stateConditions: All India; priority to deserving students

amount: ₹10,000 – ₹50,000 per year depending on education level

eligibility.qualification: Students enrolled in Class 11 or above in India
eligibility.stateConditions: All India

name: Aditya Birla Capital Scholarship

eligibility.qualification: Admitted to 1st year of UG in top institutions (IITs, NITs, IIMs, top universities)
eligibility.minPercentage: Admission to national-level premium institution is key criterion
eligibility.stateConditions: Premium institutions across India

amount: Up to ₹65,000/year + mentoring from Aditya Birla Group professionals

name: Sitaram Jindal Foundation Scholarship

eligibility.qualification: Students from 11th standard onwards pursuing education in India
eligibility.minPercentage: 55% for Girls; 65% for Boys in previous exam
eligibility.stateConditions: Priority to Karnataka, Maharashtra, Andhra Pradesh, West Bengal; all India accepted

amount: ₹500 – ₹2,000 per month depending on level (up to ₹24,000/year)
applicationProcess: Send to Sitaram Jindal Foundation Bangalore office OR apply online
renewalConditions: Annual submission of marksheet; maintain marks

name: Cummins India Scholarship (STEM Girls)

eligibility.qualification: Girl students in 1st year of Engineering or Diploma in STEM fields in Maharashtra
eligibility.stateConditions: Primarily Maharashtra (Pune focus) – partner colleges; check current availability

amount: ₹30,000 – ₹60,000 per year + mentoring + soft skills training
applicationProcess: Selected scholars receive scholarship + Cummins mentors

name: Kotak Kanya Scholarship

eligibility.qualification: Girl student; passed Class 12 scoring 85%+; enrolled in full-time professional degree (Engineering/Medicine/Law/CA/etc.)
eligibility.stateConditions: All India

amount: ₹1,50,000 per year (up to entire duration of course)

name: L'Oréal India For Young Women in Science Scholarship

eligibility.qualification: Girl students enrolled in BSc/MSc in Pure Sciences (Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Math)
eligibility.minPercentage: 85% in Class 12 or 60% in UG for PG level
eligibility.stateConditions: All India

eligibility.qualification: Students enrolled in Diploma in Engineering or ITI (trades like Mechanical, Electrical, etc.)
eligibility.stateConditions: Government polytechnics and ITIs; institution must list scholarship

name: Foundation for Excellence (FFE) Scholarship

eligibility.qualification: 1st year UG students in Engineering or Science colleges in India
eligibility.category: All categories; preference to first generation college students
eligibility.stateConditions: All India; must be in FFE-listed institution

amount: ₹20,000 – ₹30,000 per year (covers fees and living expenses)
applicationProcess: Selected scholars receive annual stipend + mentoring

name: Indian Oil Corporation Limited (IOCL) Scholarship

eligibility.qualification: Students from Class 11 to PG level
eligibility.stateConditions: All India; preference to children of IOCL employees (separate quota)

applicationProcess: Upload documents and submit before deadline

name: ONGC Scholarship

eligibility.qualification: SC/ST students admitted to 1st year of UG technical/MBA programs
eligibility.familyIncomeLimit: No fixed limit; preference to needy
eligibility.stateConditions: All India; recognized universities

applicationProcess: Download and fill ONGC scholarship form
Submit through college or directly to ONGC

name: Vidyasaarathi Portal Scholarships

eligibility.qualification: Varies by scholarship (multiple PSUs and corporates listed)
eligibility.stateConditions: All India and state-specific schemes available

documents: Varies; typically: marksheet, income certificate, Aadhaar, bank passbook

name: NSP Portal – Multiple Scholarships

eligibility.qualification: Varies by individual scholarship
eligibility.category: All categories; most schemes are caste/income specific
eligibility.stateConditions: Both central and state scholarships available on single portal

applicationProcess: Search and apply to eligible schemes
renewalConditions: Annual renewal through NSP; fresh marks required
